domain_focus: DocRED-style document-level relation extraction from Wikipedia-style text. The goal is not open-ended ontology learning. The goal is to extract named entity mentions and relation triples that match the DocRED/Wikidata property vocabulary.
abstraction_level: Benchmark-constrained extraction mode. Preserve exact surface mentions from the document as entity labels. Do not replace named entities with generic concepts or classes. Use 'Skai TV', not 'entity'. Use 'Piraeus', not 'port city'. Use 'Greece', not 'country'. Use '1st of April 2006', not 'date'. Use 'Athens', not 'geographical region'. Use 'Nova', not 'nova event'. Subjects and objects of triples must be document mentions, not abstract concepts generated from external knowledge.
priority_relations: P131 : located in the administrative territorial entity
P206 : located in or next to body of water
P1412 : languages spoken, written or signed
population_policy: Only generate triples whose predicate belongs exactly to one of the priority_relations. Do not generate open-ended predicates outside the DocRED relation vocabulary. Do not generate generic relations such as 'dubbing', 'available on', 'located in', 'largest media groups in the country', 'opted for', 'switched to', or 'uses subtitles' unless they are explicitly mapped to one of the allowed DocRED property labels. Keep subjects and objects as exact document-level mentions.
event_modeling_preference: DocRED is primarily an entity-level relation extraction benchmark. Treat persons, organizations, locations, geopolitical entities, works, dates, languages, products, and other named mentions as entities. Only create event candidates when the text clearly contains an event mention that is needed as an argument of an allowed DocRED relation.

typing_examples.explanation: Keep the exact organization mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with 'entity' or 'television network'.
Keep the exact location mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with 'port city'.
Keep the exact country mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with 'country'.
Keep the exact organization mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with a generic media group description.
Keep the exact date mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with 'date'.
Keep the exact location mention as the entity label. Do not replace it with 'geographical region'.
In DocRED, preserve the document mention. Do not reinterpret this as an astronomical nova unless the document explicitly says so.
Keep the exact organization/service mention as an entity.
Keep the exact organization mention as an entity.
If the dataset treats the demonym/language/country-related mention as an entity, preserve the surface form 'Greek'.

relation_examples.explanation: The text says Skai TV is Greek and operates in Greece. Use the allowed DocRED property label.
Piraeus is a location in Greece. Use P17 : country.
Athens is a location in Greece. Use P17 : country.
The text says Skai Group is one of the largest media groups in the country, referring to Greece.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV P159 : headquarters location Piraeus
relation_examples.explanation: The text says Skai TV is based in Piraeus.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV P159 : headquarters location Athens
relation_examples.explanation: The text says Skai TV relaunched in the Athens metropolitan area. If the dataset marks this as headquarters/location, use the DocRED label.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV P127 : owned by Skai Group
relation_examples.explanation: The text says Skai TV is part of the Skai Group. For DocRED, map this to the allowed property P127 : owned by when appropriate.

relation_examples.text: Skai TV P463 : member of Digea
relation_examples.explanation: The text says Skai TV is a member of Digea.
Digea is described as a consortium introducing digital terrestrial transmission in Greece.
Cosmote TV is mentioned in the Greek broadcasting context.

promotion_examples.explanation: For DocRED evaluation, do not promote surface mentions into abstract concepts.
For DocRED evaluation, preserve the surface mention.
For DocRED evaluation, preserve the surface mention.
For DocRED evaluation, preserve the surface mention.

negative_examples.explanation: Wrong. Do not replace a named entity with the generic label 'entity'. The label must remain 'Skai TV'.
Wrong for this benchmark. 'television network' may be a type or description, but the entity label must remain 'Skai TV'.
Wrong. Preserve the surface mention 'Piraeus'. 'port city' may appear in the description, not as the entity label.
Wrong. Preserve the surface mention 'Greece'. 'country' may be a type, not the entity label.
Wrong. Preserve the surface mention 'Athens' or 'Athens metropolitan area' depending on the document mention.
Wrong. Preserve the exact date mention as the label.
Wrong. Do not use external astronomical knowledge when the document context refers to Nova as a service/entity.
Wrong for DocRED relation extraction. 'dubbing' is not an allowed DocRED property in priority_relations.
Wrong unless the predicate is mapped exactly to an allowed DocRED property. Do not output arbitrary predicates.

negative_examples.text: Skai Group largest media groups in the country Greece
negative_examples.explanation: Wrong predicate. Convert only to an allowed relation if appropriate, such as 'P17 : country'; otherwise do not output a triple.
Wrong. Subjects and objects must be document mentions such as 'Piraeus' and 'Greece', not generic classes.
Wrong. The subject must be the exact document mention, such as 'Skai TV', not the generic word 'entity'.
Avoid generic relation labels. Use an allowed DocRED property such as 'P17 : country', 'P276 : location', or 'P131 : located in the administrative territorial entity' when explicitly supported.
Do not output the generic phrase alone if the correct DocRED property is more specific, for example 'P127 : owned by' or 'P361 : part of'.
